items.title: Schorsing voor horrortackle in België wordt ingekort
items.summary: Kévin Van den Kerkhof mist de komende drie wedstrijden van Charleroi in de Jupiler Pro League. Aanvankelijk werd een schorsing van vijf duels - waarvan één voorwaardelijk - geëist voor een flinke aanslag van de 29-jarige Algerijn.

items.title: Morocco coach quits 3 months before World Cup
items.summary: Morocco coach Walid Regragui has quit the team just months before it competes at the World Cup, he confirmed at a late-night news conference on Thursday.

items.title: Ronaldo injury 'more serious' than expected
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will travel to Madrid to undergo treatment on his right hamstring injury, Al Nassr coach Jorge Jesus confirmed on Friday.

items.title: Foxborough official: No agreement on WC funding
items.summary: A Foxborough official has insisted that no agreement has been reached over the funding of World Cup security costs, despite a commitment from organizers and the Kraft Group.

items.title: Wrexham relish 'amazing distraction' vs. Chelsea
items.summary: Wrexham captain Dominic Hyam has called Saturday's FA Cup fifth round tie at home to Chelsea an "amazing distraction" from their promotion charge as they look to make more history in the competition.

items.title: Mexico to deploy 100,000 security personnel for World Cup
items.summary: World Cup co-hosts Mexico plan to deploy nearly 100,000 security personnel to protect fans at this summer's tournament, amid ongoing cartel violence in the country.

items.title: Wolves v Liverpool: FA Cup fifth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup fifth round news from the 8pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott Wolves boss Rob Edwards speaks to TNT Sports. “It’s really important … I wouldn’t want to say this is our stronger team, we have a really tight-knit group now … really good options … we wanted to try to win both games … we picked a team on Tuesday night to try to do that and we’ve done that again tonight … we’re expecting a little bit more aggression from Liverpool … to really come at us … we’ve got real speed on the outside … [Liverpool] are going to want to prove a point tonight … we have to be right back at it … I don’t see them changing loads tactically … we feel we’ve got a good plan … we can enjoy it … [the fans] deserve something to shout about.” Wolves make six changes to the side named for the Premier League match on Tuesday night, and go big. Sam Johnstone, Angel Gomes, Jean‐Ricner Bellegarde, Tolu Arokodare, Yerson Mosquera and captain Toti Gomes are in. José Sá, Matt Doherty, Ladislav Krejčí, David Møller Wolfe, Adam Armstrong and André are out, with all bar Sá and André on tonight’s bench. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Slot kiest voor zeventienjarig toptalent Ngumoha in bekerduel met Wolves
items.summary: Vrijdagavond gaat FC Utrecht om 20.00 uur op bezoek bij hekkensluiter Heracles Almelo. Ook in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie wordt gevoetbald en Liverpool, Bayern München, Real Madrid, Napoli en Paris Saint-Germain spelen ook. Mis niets met de VI Live.

items.title: While Trump monetises war, Iran women’s team deliver great act of sporting heroism | Barney Ronay
items.summary: In refusing to sing the national anthem these athletes have placed themselves in grave danger while Gianni Infantino sides with the American war machine A small but telling detail from a vast and baffling chain of events. You probably saw the footage of Donald Trump’s declaration of war on Iran two weeks ago, a piece of history played out in real time, a moment where the inevitable violent deaths of thousands of people were in effect announced. In the video Trump is shown propped up at his plinth, using that sing-song intonation he employs to appear cod-statesmanlike, faux-grave, but sounding instead like a semi-sentient robot vacuum cleaner in the seconds before it runs out of battery life. To the great people of Iran. America is backing you. Don’t go outside. It’s very dangerous out there. We will for the foreseeable future be bombing you to freedom. Continue reading...

items.title: Six Nations LIVE! Ireland and Wales kick off Round 4 in Dublin
Hoe strateeg Fàbregas zich bij Como toont als grote trainersbelofte
items.summary: Cesc Fàbregas (38) werd met Spanje wereldkampioen en won twee EK’s en verzamelde ook als clubspeler talloze prijzen. Als trainer begon hij bij Como op nul in de Serie B om zich op te werken tot een van de interessantste trainers van Europa.

items.title: Heracles ontvangt viermiljoenste bezoeker ooit in Asito Stadion
items.summary: Heracles Almelo neemt het vrijdagavond op tegen FC Utrecht en de hekkensluiter van de Eredivisie ontvangt daarbij een bijzondere toeschouwer. De viermiljoenste fan ooit kwam het Asito Stadion ingedruppeld.

items.title: Ronaldo to travel to Spain for hamstring treatment
items.summary: Portugal captain Cristiano Ronaldo will travel to Spain for treatment on a hamstring injury that is "more serious" than first thought.

items.title: Eerste vrouwelijke hoofdtrainer in Europese profvoetbal dwingt nieuw contract af
items.summary: FC Ingolstadt 04 zet de samenwerking met hoofdtrainster Sabrina Wittmann voort. Het contract van de 34-jarige Duitse is opengebroken en verlengd, zo meldt de Derde Bundesliga-club op zijn website. Wittmann is de eerste - en momenteel ook de enige - vrouwelijke hoofdtrainer, die actief is in het mannenprofvoetbal.

items.title: Igor Tudor is flailing in the face of panic with Spurs staring at apocalypse now
items.summary: Relegation fears are sharpening while interim head coach grasps for solutions at a club hampered by inhibition, rage and injuries Igor Tudor’s messaging was always going to be key. All eyes and ears were on the interim Tottenham head coach on Thursday night and how he would react; the tone he would look to set. Would there be another blast for the players? Goodness knows, the material was there. It had been another impossibly awful occasion at the club’s home stadium, another defeat – this one by Crystal Palace. Spurs cannot buy a Premier League win at the Temple of Gloom; they have two all season, the basis for the worst home record in the division. As relegation fears sharpen to an incredibly uncomfortable point, the emotions in the stands ranged from apathy to anger. A lot of anger. Continue reading...

items.title: Moment of truth for England as Scotland bid to keep Six Nations title race alive
items.summary: Rome’s modern amphitheatre awaits a wounded England, their Six Nations campaign fraying and Italy sensing a rare chance to rewrite history, while at Murrayfield France can put both hands on a trophy Scotland still harbour hopes of claiming for the first time...

items.title: Baumgardner to defend titles against Shin over three-minute rounds
items.summary: Alycia Baumgardner will fight top contender Bo Mi Re Shin over 10 three-minute rounds, the same round length as in men’s boxing, in New York City on April 17.

items.title: Hurrell and Manby clash after tense UK Open match!
items.summary: James Hurrell gets heated after falling to Charlie Manby, who advances to round four of the 2026 UK Open. X Credit: @OfficialPDC.

items.title: 'What is a foul? What is a contact?' - Nuno calls for clarity
items.summary: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo is calling for clarity on what constitutes a foul at set-pieces, warning current levels of grappling and blocking are "almost crazy".

items.title: Premier League chiefs hold talks on rise of set pieces amid concern over quality
items.summary: Top flight directors discuss holding in penalty area Set pieces account for 27.1% of Premier League goals The Premier League’s sporting directors have held talks about the ubiquity of set-piece goals and holding in the penalty area, amid concerns these trends are damaging the spectacle. Liverpool’s head coach, Arne Slot, said this week that his “football heart doesn’t like it”, when asked about the growing importance of set pieces, which have been responsible for 27.1% of Premier League goals this season, excluding penalties. Continue reading...

items.title: Leeds condemn 'disappointing' Ramadan boos but cite 'mitigating circumstances'
items.summary: Leeds have condemned the "unexpected and disappointing" booing from fans during a scheduled pause for players to break their Ramadan fast in last weekend's defeat to Man City - but set out a number of "mitigating circumstances" in their defence.

items.title: Manchester United and Newcastle defend 5% price rises on season tickets
items.summary: Supporter groups’ pleas for price freezes ignored Both clubs insist hikes needed to compete on pitch Manchester United and Newcastle have increased season-ticket prices by 5% for 2026-27. While both clubs insist the rises are necessary to achieve success on the pitch, their respective supporter groups have strongly criticised the above-inflation hikes. Season tickets at Old Trafford had previously been frozen for 11 consecutive campaigns, but this is now the fourth successive year they have been increased. The club said: “We have a clear objective to return Manchester United to the top of domestic and European football. We want to keep investing in the team and improving our facilities so fans get the best possible experience. We also need to make sure the club stays financially sustainable taking into account inflation and rising costs. Continue reading...

items.title: Who is to blame for Spurs' chaotic descent towards relegation?
items.summary: BBC Sport's chief football writer Phil McNulty examines who - or what - is to blame for Spurs' crisis as they fight to avoid relegation.

items.title: 'Stick with the plan' - Patience key for England, says Wiegman
items.summary: England manager Sarina Wiegman says it is "very important" her team are patient if goals do not come quickly in their 2027 Women's World Cup qualifier against Iceland on Saturday.

items.title: Ranked: The 10 worst Premier League teams that spent too much money on being bad
items.summary: Tottenham are on pace for Premier League relegation despite spending lots of cash. Where do they rank among the worst teams relative to spending?

items.title: "Je trouve ça important" : l'ajustement de Jérémy Taravel qui a libéré les attaquants d'Anderlecht
items.summary: Dimanche, Anderlecht se déplacera au Club de Bruges. Au-delà des retrouvailles entre Jérémy Taravel et Ivan Leko, la rencontre s'annonce comme un beau challenge pour les Mauves.

items.title: Stijn Stijnen legt budget van Patro op tafel en vergelijkt dat met beloftenploegen
items.summary: Patro Eisden pakte tegen Jong Genk, Club NXT en Jong KAA Gent 'slechts' vier op negen. Zaterdag is RSCA Futures de volgende tegenstander.

items.title: LIVE : la RAAL peut-elle se donner de l'air et enterrer les rêves de l'Antwerp ?
items.summary: La RAAL reçoit l'Antwerp ce vendredi soir en ouverture de la 28e journée de Jupiler Pro League. Le Great Old doit gagner pour encore rêver de top 6.

items.title: "C'est vrai dans une certaine mesure" : Rik De Mil réagit au constat implacable de l'un de ses joueurs
items.summary: La Gantoise disputera ce week-end un match crucial dans la lutte pour le top 6 face à Malines. Rik De Mil croit en les chances de ses hommes après une bonne semaine d'entraînement.

items.title: LIVE: Niet Vandeplas maar Hamdaoui vervangt Janssen tegen La Louvière
items.summary: De 28e speeldag van de Jupiler Pro League trekt zich op gang met een duel tussen La Louvière en Antwerp FC. Als Antwerp wint, maakt het nog een waterkansje op de top 6. Grijpen ze die laatste strohalm?
